questions.question: What kind of exposure does project based learning primarily provide to students regarding a problem?
questions.explanation: Project based learning gives a thorough practical exposure to students regarding a problem upon which the project is based.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which of the following is a skill that students learn through project based learning in groups?
questions.explanation: Projects are developed generally in groups where students can learn various skills such as working together, problem solving, decision making, and investigating activities.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the final step involved in the problem-solving phase of project based learning?
options: Formulating the problem into small modules
Applying the mechanism to each module
Integrating the solution of all the modules
questions.explanation: Project based learning involves analyzing, formulating into modules, applying mechanisms, and then integrating the solution of all the modules to arrive at the complete solution.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which project execution approach involves dividing a project into various manageable modules with well-defined tasks?
questions.explanation: A structured or a modular approach to a project means that a project is divided into various manageable modules and each of the modules has a well-defined task to be performed.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the approaches mentioned for solving projects?
questions.explanation: There are several approaches to execute a project such as modular approach, top down approach and bottom up approach.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: According to the steps in project based learning, what is the first step?
questions.explanation: The first step in project based learning is the 'Identification of a project'.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Where does a project idea typically come from in the context of project based learning?
questions.explanation: The project idea may come through any real-life situation.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What type of projects are students explicitly encouraged to undertake?
questions.explanation: Students must be encouraged to undertake interdisciplinary projects.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: During the 'Defining a plan' step, who needs to be identified if there are several project members?
questions.explanation: Normally for any kind of project, there are several project members involved in it. One project leader has to be identified.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: To obtain a better solution while defining a plan, one should always think of:
questions.explanation: To obtain a better solution, one should always think of the extreme situations.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the third step in the project based learning process?
options: Fixing of a time frame and processing
questions.explanation: The third step is 'Fixing of a time frame and processing'.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Every project must be well structured, and at the same time, it must be __________ in its time frame.
questions.explanation: Every project must be well structured and at the same time it must be flexible in its time frame.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: When project participants get stuck with a particular process, who primarily helps in monitoring and guiding the project besides the project leader?
questions.explanation: While it is essential that the project leader should ensure monitoring of the project, the guide teacher also helps in monitoring the project.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Where can guidance be obtained if participants find it impossible to proceed further?
options: From various resources such as books, websites and experts
questions.explanation: In such a case, they need guidance, which can be obtained from various resources such as books, websites and experts in the field.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: The outcome of a project can be:
questions.explanation: The outcome can be single, or it can be multiple.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: The output of a project can be peer-reviewed and modified based on the feedback from:
options: The guide teacher or other users
questions.explanation: The output of a project can be peer reviewed and can be modified as per the feedback from the guide teacher or other users.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the term for efforts made by individuals collectively to accomplish a task?
questions.explanation: Efforts made by individuals collectively to accomplish a task is called teamwork.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which sports example is explicitly used in the chapter to illustrate the concept of teamwork?
questions.explanation: Take an example of a cricket team. We find that even if a bowler bowls a good ball but if the fielder cannot take a catch then the wicket cannot be taken.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a component of teamwork?
questions.explanation: The components of teamwork are Communicate with Others, Listen to Others, Share with Others, Respect for Others, Help Others, and Participate. Competing is not one of them.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which component of teamwork involves arranging group meetings or using e-mails and telephones?
questions.explanation: Such communication can be done via e-mails, telephones or by arranging group meetings.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Why is effective communication necessary in teamwork?
options: To understand each other and sort out problems
questions.explanation: This helps the team members to understand each other and sort out their problems to achieve the goal effectively.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which component of teamwork helps members understand each other's ideas by actively paying attention in group meetings?
questions.explanation: It is necessary to understand the ideas of others while executing a job together. This can be achieved when the team members listen to each other.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Ideas, images, and tools need to be transferred among members under which component of teamwork?
questions.explanation: Ideas, images and tools need to be shared with each other in order to perform a job. Sharing is an important component of teamwork.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the primary reason for a team member well versed in a certain area to share their expertise?
options: To effectively achieve the goal within the time frame
To take over the project leadership
To minimize other members' workload completely
questions.explanation: Any member of the team who is well versed in a certain area should share the expertise and experience with others to effectively achieve the goal within the time frame.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which component of teamwork stresses that not giving due consideration to someone's views may cause problems in project execution?
questions.explanation: Not respecting the views of a particular member may cause problems and that particular team member may not give his best.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Is it possible to obtain help from people who are not a part of the team in order to accomplish a job?
options: Yes, sometimes help from people outside the team is obtained
Only if the guide teacher does it
No, teamwork strictly limits outside help
questions.explanation: Sometimes help from people who are not a part of the team is also obtained in order to accomplish a job.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Why should every team member take an active participation in completing the project?
options: So they feel their importance in the team
To bypass the project leader's authority
So the project takes longer to complete
questions.explanation: Also, every member should take an active participation so that they feel their importance in the team.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the 'Automation of Order Processing in a Restaurant' project description, what is the name of the restaurant?
questions.explanation: A new restaurant "Stay Healthy" is coming up in your locality.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the restaurant automation project, what are the two types of Login options specified?
questions.explanation: There should be two types of Login options - one for the manager of the joint and other for the customer.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the restaurant automation project, where are the kiosks meant to be placed for placing the order?
questions.explanation: Kiosk(s) running the software for customers will be placed at reception for placing the order.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What information must the customer enter to place an order in the restaurant automation software?
questions.explanation: To place orders, customers will enter Item Code(s) and quantity desired.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: After placing an order in the restaurant kiosk, what is displayed to the customer?
options: A soft copy of the bill having an Order Number
questions.explanation: After placing an order, a soft copy of the bill will be displayed on the kiosk, having an Order Number.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the restaurant automation project, what constitutes the unique identification for every bill?
options: Combination of date and order number of the day
Item code of the first order
questions.explanation: Every bill will have a unique identification (such as combination of date, and order number of the day) and should be saved in the data file/database.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What does the Order Number start from every day in the restaurant project?
options: It continues from the previous day's last number
questions.explanation: Order Number starts from 1 every day.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: For the manager login in the Restaurant automation project, which specific report generation is explicitly requested?
options: Summary of the Sales made on a Day
questions.explanation: For Manager login-provision for... generation of following report is desired. A Report giving Summary of the Sales made on a Day.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In Project Title 2, what language is suggested to implement the puzzle solving game?
questions.explanation: Implement a puzzle solving game in Python.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the 'Development of a Puzzle' game project, what happens if a player clicks a cell containing a bomb?
options: The player loses a life but continues
questions.explanation: For clearing the board, the player will click a cell on the board, if the cell contains a bomb, the game finishes.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the puzzle project, what does a cell reveal if it does NOT contain a bomb?
options: A number giving a clue about the number of bombs hidden in adjacent cells
The location of all other bombs
questions.explanation: If the cell does not contain a bomb, then the cell reveals a number giving a clue about the number of bombs hidden in adjacent cells.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What existing game are students advised to play to understand the puzzle project properly?
questions.explanation: Before you start coding the game, play any Minesweeper game five times. This will help you in proper understanding of your project.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: To reduce the complexity of the puzzle game program, what fixed grid size and number of bombs are suggested?
questions.explanation: To reduce the complexity of the program you can fix the grid size to 6x6 and number of bombs to 6.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the 'Match the Sum' educational game project, what is the specified age group of the kids whose mathematical skills are targeted?
questions.explanation: As a club member, you are given the responsibility of identifying ways to improve mathematical skills of kids, in the age group of 5-7 years.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the 'Match the Sum' game, what is the target sum that students need to form?
questions.explanation: Match the Sum will hone summing skills of student(s), by allowing them to form number 10 by adding 2/3 digits.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the 'Match the Sum' game, how many cells are displayed on the screen at a time?
questions.explanation: Display a list of 15 cells on screen, where each cell can hold a digit (1 to 9).
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What values can each cell hold in the 'Match the Sum' game?
questions.explanation: Display a list of 15 cells on screen, where each cell can hold a digit (1 to 9).
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the 'Match the Sum' game, from which end are the randomly generated digits placed in the list?
questions.explanation: Randomly generate a digit at a time and place it in the list from the right end.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What happens to the already existing digits in the 'Match the Sum' game when a new digit is added to the list?
options: They are shifted right by one cell
They are shifted left by one cell
questions.explanation: Already existing digits, will be shifted left, by one cell, with every new addition of digits' in the list.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: To play the 'Match the Sum' game, how many digits are students allowed to type at a time to form the sum?
questions.explanation: For playing the game, students' will be allowed to type 2/3 digits (one at a time) currently displayed in the list of cells.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: If the sum of the typed digits in the 'Match the Sum' game is 10, what action should the program take?
options: Remove those digits from the list of cells
Add another 10 to the score immediately
questions.explanation: If the sum of those digits is 10, then those digits should get removed from the list of cells.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Until when does the 'Match the Sum' game continue?
options: Till there is an empty cell to insert a digit in the list
Till the list contains only the number 10
questions.explanation: Game will continue till there is an empty cell to insert a digit in the list of cells.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which of the following is an exception handling requirement specifically noted for the 'Match the Sum' project?
options: Take care of the situation when digits displayed do not add up to 10
Stop the game if the mouse is clicked
questions.explanation: Note: Do take care of the situation when digits displayed in a list of cells do not add up to 10.
questions.subject: Computer Science
